content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build a browser-based Yew/Rust/WASM Pascal Compiler IDE for COR24 p-code VM. Read-only Pascal source and pre-compiled p-code assembly side by side, with link/assemble/run entirely in WASM. Phase 1: scaffold Yew project with Trunk, wire cor24-emulator + pa24r + pl24r dependencies, build.rs to pre-assemble pvm.s. Phase 2: demo registry — embed .pas + .spc pairs via include_str!(), demo selector dropdown. Phase 3: UI layout — four-panel grid (Pascal source, p-code assembly, output, hardware), Catppuccin Mocha theme. Phase 4: Link & Run pipeline — pl24r linking, pa24r assembly, loader (load .p24 at 0x010000, relocate data refs), batch execution. Phase 5: I/O and hardware — UART output display, UART input field, LED D2 indicator, S2 switch toggle. Phase 6: status bar + polish — Ready/Running/Exited states, instruction count, binary size, Stop button.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-scaffold-yew-trunk [legacy]: Scaffold the Yew/Trunk/WASM project. Study web-dv24r's Cargo.toml, build.rs, ...
  [x] 002-002-demo-registry [legacy]: Build the demo registry: embed .pas + .spc pairs via include_str\!(). Study w...
  [x] 003-003-ui-layout [production]: Phase 3: Four-panel grid layout with Catppuccin Mocha theme. Study web-dv24r'...
  [x] 004-004-link-and-run [production]: Phase 4: Link & Run pipeline. Study web-dv24r's execution model for pattern r... <-- YOU ARE HERE

=== CURRENT STEP: 004-004-link-and-run [production] ===
Status: completed
Description: Phase 4: Link & Run pipeline. Study web-dv24r's execution model for pattern r...

=== PROMPT ===
Phase 4: Link & Run pipeline. Study web-dv24r's execution model for pattern reference. Implement: (1) pl24r linking of demo .spc with runtime .spc files, (2) pa24r assembly of linked output to .p24 binary, (3) Loader — load pvm.s machine code, load .p24 at 0x010000, relocate data segment references, (4) Batch execution loop using cor24-emulator EmulatorCore — 50K instructions per tick via requestAnimationFrame or setInterval to prevent browser blocking, (5) Wire the Link & Run button to trigger this pipeline for the currently selected demo. Display output in the output console panel. Verify trunk build succeeds and demos can be linked, assembled, and executed in the browser.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

content.content:
Workflow CLI for keeping AI agents on track

Usage: agentrail [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  init      Initialize a new saga
  setup     Bootstrap a project: create saga, CLAUDE.md, and register domain
  add       Add a step to the saga (for maintenance mode or ad-hoc tasks)
  status    Show current saga state
  next      Output current step prompt and context for a fresh agent session
  begin     Mark the current step as in-progress
  complete  Complete current step, optionally define next step
  plan      View or update the saga plan
  history   Show all step summaries
  distill   Distill trajectories into a skill document
  run-loop  Auto-execute deterministic steps, pause at agent steps
  abort     Mark current step as blocked
  help      Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --saga <SAGA>  Path to the project directory (default: current directory) [default: .]
  -h, --help         Print help
  -V, --version      Print version

content.content:
Add a step to the saga (for maintenance mode or ad-hoc tasks)

Usage: agentrail add [OPTIONS] --slug <SLUG> --prompt <PROMPT>

Options:
      --slug <SLUG>            Step slug (short name)
      --prompt <PROMPT>        Step prompt: text, file path, or "-" for stdin
      --role <ROLE>            Step role (production, deterministic, validation, meta) [default: production]
      --task-type <TASK_TYPE>  Task type for skill/trajectory lookup
  -h, --help                   Print help

input.command: agentrail add --slug 005-io-and-hardware --prompt "Phase 5: I/O and hardware. Implement: (1) UART output display — show TX bytes in the output console panel with auto-scroll, (2) UART input field — text input with Send button, feed bytes to emulator RX queue, (3) LED D2 indicator — read memory-mapped address 0xFF0200, display green glow when lit, (4) S2 switch toggle — button to toggle switch state, write to memory-mapped address. (5) Wire all I/O to the existing batch execution loop. Verify with demos that produce output (Hello World) and demos that read input (Countdown). Phase 6: status bar + polish — Ready/Running/Exited states in status display, instruction count and binary size stats in hardware panel, Stop button to halt execution. Verify trunk build succeeds and all features work end-to-end."

content.text: Now let me read the CSS file and check what's missing.

Most features are already implemented. I see two gaps:

1. **S2 switch** - `ToggleSwitch` handler doesn't write to the memory-mapped address
2. **Output auto-scroll** - no auto-scroll when new output appears

Let me check the S2 switch address from the reference project and verify the build works.
